theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Industrials (+1.13%) and Health Care (+0.75%) led the market today, while Consumer Discretionary (-3.45%) and Communication Svcs (-2.32%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -2.32% versus -0.30% for defensives and -0.09% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained -0.90% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -2.06%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up -0.83%, outperforming large caps. The VIX rose 16.05% to 19.38, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.83% to $4,052.00, crude oil was little changed at $92.33, and bitcoin slipped 2.07% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
